title: mammillaria nelsonii
intro: Mammillaria nelsonii, also known as the Nelson's pincushion cactus, is a type of small, globular cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Mammillaria nelsonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and grinding the stems and roots, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically in the form of an ointment or salve.
